rationale: Guaranteed stock baseline on the Mac mini before autonomous modifications begin.
title: Establish baseline

rationale: Baseline memory was already low, so this run spends the budget on more optimizer steps by removing avoidable host synchronization. It defaults MLX eager materialization off on the common single-microbatch path, keeps it available for chunked accumulation, delays train loss scalar extraction until a line will actually be logged, and removes one unused loop variable.
title: Single-Microbatch Sync Cleanup

rationale: Build on the current single-microbatch win by turning unused wallclock slack into more optimizer updates. The last run finished around 579.5s with a 90s reserve, so the script is leaving training time on the table. Dropping the default reserve to 72s should reclaim roughly 15-20s without crowding final serialization/eval, and shrinking `TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS` from 8192 to 6144 should raise update count materially while staying on the fast single-microbatch path. `TRAIN_LOG_EVERY` is relaxed to 50 to avoid spending the extra steps on logging overhead.
title: Smaller Batch And Tighter Reserve

rationale: Build on the current small-batch script by spending the last stretch of train time on the exact final int8/fp16 roundtrip path instead of optimizing only the float model. The added late quantization-aware snaps should reduce train-to-final mismatch on the metric we actually score, and the longer warmdown gives those snapped steps room to settle without changing the launcher contract.
title: Late Quantization-Aware Snap And Longer Warmdown

rationale: The current best run already improved the endgame by snapping weights through the final int8 roundtrip. This change attacks the same objective more directly by reducing quantization error itself: large 2D tensors now use per-row grouped scales across columns instead of a single scale for the whole row. That should preserve more post-training signal after the exact int8+zlib roundtrip, while the extra scale metadata stays well within the current 16MB artifact headroom. The late quant-aware roundtrips automatically become more faithful because they now use the stronger grouped quantizer too.
title: Row-Group Int8 Quantization Alignment

rationale: The current best run leaves about 3.16MB of compressed headroom under the 16MB cap, so this change spends some of that budget on the tensors most likely to dominate post-quantization error: the final decoder block is kept in FP16 and the last two blocks keep their output projections in FP16. That directly targets `final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_bpb` without slowing the hot training loop, while keeping the existing late quant-aware roundtrip schedule intact.
title: Late-Decoder FP16 Quantization Rescue

rationale: Run_0006 already spent almost the entire 16MB budget on the most sensitive late decoder weights, so this iteration avoids another FP16-size increase and instead targets the remaining int8 error directly. The current per-row clip percentile is so high that 512/1024-wide rows are effectively quantized by their maxima, which wastes int8 range on rare outliers. Lowering the default int8 clip percentile to 99.8 makes clipping actually active on those matrix rows, and extending plus densifying the late quant-aware phase gives the model more chances to adapt to that sharper final quantizer before the exact roundtrip evaluation.
title: Per-Row Outlier Clipping With Denser Late Quant-Aware Snaps

rationale: Builds on the current best late-roundtrip and FP16-tail rescue by changing the endgame optimizer behavior instead of repeating clipping or row-group experiments. Once quant-aware training becomes active, matrix and embedding learning rates are reduced more aggressively than scalar/control rates so the model can settle into the exact final quantized manifold while small float controls still adapt. This targets final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact directly with minimal complexity and no metric-pipeline changes.
title: Quant-Aware Late LR Rebalance

rationale: This keeps the run-0008 quant-aware and tail-FP16 quantization wins, but pivots to a new idea category: use 512-token training windows with a larger 8192-token batch and 16384-token validation batch. On a 600s M4 budget, shorter sequences should materially cut per-step attention cost and allow many more optimizer steps/tokens, while the model still preserves the same parameterization and late quantization-aware behavior. I also drop the fixed final-eval reserve floor from 72s to 48s because the shorter-context eval path should be much cheaper, which should buy back additional training time without touching the hard wallclock cap.
title: Half-Context Higher-Token Budget

rationale: The strongest path so far is late quant-aware tuning plus selective late-decoder FP16 rescue. This run keeps compounding that: it resets Muon and Adam exactly when the first quant-aware roundtrip starts so stale optimizer state does not fight the quantized basin, and it spends a bit more size budget on the most quantization-sensitive tail blocks by default. The change is cheap in wallclock, directly targets final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_bpb, and stays under the 1500-line limit.
title: Quant-Aware Phase Reset With Deeper Tail FP16 Rescue

rationale: The current best script wins by expanding late FP16 rescue, but that pushes the quantized artifact over 16MB. This change keeps the quant-aware phase reset and reallocates the same approximate FP16 payload budget used by the last valid run: drop the always-float token embedding, keep the final block fully in FP16, keep the last two projection pairs, and add penultimate-block QKV in FP16. The hypothesis is that late attention weights are more roundtrip-sensitive than the embedding table, so this should recover more of run 0010's gain while staying near run 0008's artifact size. I also removed a redundant float-keep branch.
title: Budget-Neutral Tail QKV FP16 Rescue

rationale: Builds directly on the best late LR rebalance path by making the first quant-aware step a real phase change: roundtrip once at phase start, reset Muon/Adam state so stale optimizer moments do not fight the quantized weights, then continue the existing periodic roundtrips. I also removed a redundant float-keep branch to keep the script lean and size-neutral.
title: Quant-Aware Phase-Start Reset
